item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image used in this article is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0), as it comes from Wikimedia Commons under CC BY 2.0.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is pretty good.

Just one thing, I'm not sure that the phrase "cognitive thought" means very much.

Perhaps something more like cogent, intelligent, cohesive might be along the lines of what you're looking for.

item.title: Staff Post - Closed
item.content:encoded:
Ketermaster, I will make it very clear that if you want to use images on forum, put them under a collapsible. This is for the benefit of those using mobile phones to browse the site.

And as per the statement from SpookBee, I hope you abide by it. Anymore could be construed as harassment.

P.S. May a mod put this under a collapsible?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Staff notice: this user is permanently banned for trolling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's not amusing. It isn't funny.

To be blunt, your being an incredibly generic troll - throwing shit at people and then using the bullshit excuse of "It's just a prank bro" when people respond negatively.

Stop.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Staff notice: this user is permanently banned for trolling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When someone asks you not to comment on their stuff, it's generally polite not to do what they ask you not to do.

Please refrain from commenting on my articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dude.
Don't be an asshole.
That shit gets you nowhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: She was being serious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Staff notice: this user is permanently banned for trolling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice addition. I definitely feel this this could even be longer, as if it could use even more. Keep up the good work

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did you forget to change the text on the interview log when you uncollapse it? It says 'title for hiding text.'

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually I'm gonna take some notes on that… I'm, essentially, avoiding going too far and sticking with a generally-okay skip, but I'll more-than-likely be expanding on it in the future, in hoping that it survives long enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not really sure this is a valid judgement. Professionalism doesn't hinge on the ability to speak impeccably unhesitant English.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, not to mention, but surely precautions would be taken to make sure the SCP wouldn't infect the researcher, or any other on-site personnel? Seems… unfoundation-like overall. The concept still has some good potential, I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't follow the interview at all. Not only is it unprofessional, the interviewer's not even asking sensible questions, and then there's random death at the end just because. :/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the idea, but the interview feels too unprofessional with the umms and uhhs. No-voting for now until that is changed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
With some grammatical fixes and maybe a test log, this could be a pretty sound article…

Though I don't really follow the idea of… Tree root parasitic street performers.

But I think that the positives outweigh the negatives.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Might be obvious, but...
- Might be obvious, but...

Is it just me, or does it feel like the "original" instance isn't the original, but just another -A instance from a prior infection event? At least, that's the feeling I get from 2868 being a humanoid thing made of random material with weird additions/subtractions that has root-feet that let it infect people and turn them into humanoid things made of random materials with weird additions/subtractions that have root-feet that let them create further instances in the same manner. Except for 2868 (apparently) being discovered sans an infecting agent, as none is mentioned, it is functionally the same as a -A instance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the interviews got too many umms and uhhs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
. In addition, infected hosts display a strong desire to interact with highly populat

Does this belong in the closing statement or somewhere else? Seemed like a non sequitur.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perhaps if the "everyone should care about art" evolves into "every one of us is a work of art, and it takes the right sculptor to see that potential," then we might be on to something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a bunch of disparate, random elements kind of thrown together without much regard for anything resembling an overall idea. Blade armed faceless humanoid with roots for feet turns people into street performers by poking them with her roots. This very much feels like something that came out of a random idea generator, and the problem with those is that there's no chance with those to make something that'll really affect a reader beyond "huh, that's kind of weird, I guess."

I concur with Roget that the interview is filler; nothing in there gave us information beyond what could have been read already from the description. It suffers further from the fact that the exchange is kind of silly with the asking the subject their favorite color etc., and the dialogue is stilted and unnatural sounding.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Saw this I the chat earlier and liked it,
I see it on the site today and upvote. :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm so proud to finally have contributed to the wiki, and that this is what I decided to make my first ever article… In honesty I much prefer sticking with simple(For now anyways), that way I can keep writing okay articles until I'm confident enough to write something really out there :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm so glad you posted this! It's simple, but I loved the concept from the beginning and that's definitely enough for me to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Neutral vote. Although I heavily enjoyed lines such as "The floor of its cell is comprised of solid plate steel to prevent SCP-2868 from spreading via roots to other containment cells" it didn't add up to much in the end and the interview didn't add anything that wasn't already in the description. This article needs that next step, something to make this all come together and not be disparate elements. I'm not sure what that would be, though. Maybe what its eventual goal in assimilating all of these folks is.
